baseline.notes: All runs #35-38b plateaued at F1 ~0.39 on the Rush game. Run #39 tests generalization on a DIFFERENT 2026 game (Seattle Reign U15 vs Westside Metros, 2026-03-14) to determine if the ceiling is game-specific or pipeline-general. Same gate config as Run #38b (ball chain linking + GK proximity + trajectory). Same team (Seattle Reign 2011 GA) as several other GT'd games so camera/rig setup should be similar.

targets.notes: Success criterion is informational, not metric: does the pipeline produce sensible per-type detection distributions on a different game? If F1 is similar to Rush (~0.39) and per-type P/R patterns match, the ceiling is real and pipeline-general. If F1 is wildly different, the Rush game has some specific property (camera angle, ball visibility) that caps detection quality. Offsets are estimates — expect eval noise; trust per-type distributions over overall F1.

notes: Run #39 — generalization test on Seattle Reign vs Westside Metros (2026-03-14, U15). Same feature commit as Run #38b (4eb6546 — ball chain linking). Offsets: VIDEO_OFFSET=420 (est), half2_start=3720 (est), half2_game_offset=2400 (U15 = 40-min halves, not 45 min). Tolerance widened to 90s to absorb offset error. Match colors are placeholders (don't affect F1, only GK reel assignment). Analysis focus: per-type detection counts + distributions, not overall F1.
